name: Grok 4.3
archDisplay: Mixture-of-Experts (size undisclosed); always-on reasoning with configurable effort (none/low/medium/high, default low)
note: Default/low reasoning-effort configuration of xAI's (now SpaceXAI's) April 2026 flagship. 1M-token context, native video input, always-on reasoning defaulting to the cheapest 'low' effort tier. Predecessor to Grok 4.5; xAI did not publish a full first-party benchmark table alongside launch.

results.notes: Agents' Last Exam (ALE-CLI, Overall leaderboard), Grok CLI harness, no effort listed ('--'): Pass rate 6.6% / Score 20.1% (rank 48). No-effort-label row maps to this catalog entry's default config (effortLabel=None, isDefaultConfig=true), distinct from the separate 'Grok 4.3 (High)' row/entry. Score component used per corpus convention.

verificationNotes: Release date April 30, 2026 (API GA; app rollout ~April 17) corroborated by VentureBeat, TheAIRankings, and Requesty. Pricing $1.25/$2.50 per Mtok (with $0.20 cached-input rate) corroborated by 3 independent sources (VentureBeat, TheAIRankings, Requesty). 1M context window corroborated by TheAIRankings and Requesty. Reasoning effort tiers (none/low/medium/high, default low) corroborated by OpenRouter model page. No independent benchmark results are attached to this (default/low-effort) entry — the scores I found (GPQA 90.1, tau2-bench-telecom 97.7) all come from Artificial Analysis's model page, which is titled '(high)', so I attributed those to the Grok 4.3 (High) sibling entry instead rather than guess they apply to the default tier.
